what_it_does: Records a channel-memory attachment entry that links an image resource to the design-source channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
standouts: Target uses an image:// URI scheme pointing to design-source/vt-architects/p-4
File is a minimal metadata stub with no descriptive caption or context
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Follows a ledger-style attachment metadata convention
concerns: No caption, title, or description for the image
Target URI is opaque without external resolution
No indication of who added it or why
notes: A bare attachment record that points an image asset at the design-source channel.
questions: Is the image:// URI resolvable and where is the asset stored?
How is this attachment meant to be rendered or consumed?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record an image attachment, and the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: Catalog an image asset associated with the design-source channel so it can be retrieved alongside related channel memory.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme in the target field.
contribution: Provides a typed, timestamped pointer from a channel memory entry to a specific image resource.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-c709efeb in the design-source channel links to image://design-source/vt-architects/p-4 and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: Track an image attachment for the design-source channel.
